refined_fr.refined_fr: Enable measurement and reporting of material throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the system's capability to measure/report throughput metrics, and (2) the achievement of a customer-defined target. The latter is a success criterion dependent on operational context, training, and external factors beyond system design. The refined FR isolates the system's functional obligation—providing accurate, timely throughput data—which is solution-neutral and testable independent of target achievement. The target itself belongs in acceptance criteria or KPI definitions, not in the FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct instrumented trials under representative operating conditions. Measure reported throughput (m³/h or ton/h) against independent ground-truth measurements (e.g., load cells, volume surveys, GPS-based tracking). Verify accuracy, latency, and availability of the reporting interface across the agreed evaluation period. Document measurement uncertainty and compare against acceptance thresholds.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: Acquire real-time material throughput data (volume or mass per time interval).
FR-NGHE-001.2: Calculate and aggregate throughput metrics from acquired data.
FR-NGHE-001.3: Communicate throughput metrics to authorized stakeholders (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator) with defined latency and format.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire instantaneous flow rate via continuous sensing of material stream properties (mass or volume per unit time) and integrate over evaluation period to compute cumulative throughput
DP2: Measure discrete material batches (count, mass, or volume per batch) at regular intervals and extrapolate to throughput rate over evaluation period
DP3: Monitor material level change in a bounded reservoir (input hopper or output stockpile) over time and infer net throughput from level differential and material density
DP4: Combine multiple independent measurement modalities (e.g., batch counting + continuous sensing) with cross-validation logic to report throughput with confidence bounds
DP5: Establish a calibrated reference baseline (e.g., manual tally or certified third-party measurement) and report relative throughput variance against baseline over evaluation period
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Acquire instantaneous flow rate via continuous sensing of material stream properties (mass or volume per unit time) and integrate over evaluation period to compute cumulative throughput

potential_sources: Sensor placement and material stream geometry: continuous sensing requires stable, representative sampling point; coupling risk if stream geometry or flow profile changes during operation
Calibration drift: instantaneous flow rate sensing may require periodic recalibration; coupling to maintenance schedule and operational downtime
Environmental factors: material density, moisture, temperature may affect sensor accuracy; coupling to ambient conditions if not compensated
Integration method: numerical integration over time introduces dependency on sampling frequency and clock synchronization; low coupling if sampling rate is decoupled from reporting interval
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is preferred because it decouples the measurement logic (continuous acquisition + integration) from batch-specific variability and allows real-time reporting. Coupling risks are manageable through sensor placement discipline and calibration protocols. DP2 introduces coupling to batch size uniformity; DP3 couples to reservoir geometry and material compaction; DP4 adds complexity without reducing fundamental measurement uncertainty; DP5 is auxiliary and should complement rather than replace primary measurement.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Material stream is accessible for continuous sensing without disrupting operations. Risk: If access is limited or sensing introduces friction/blockage, throughput measurement may alter the phenomenon being measured.

Assumption: Sensor output is stable and repeatable over the evaluation period. Risk: Sensor drift, fouling, or calibration loss could cause reported throughput to diverge from actual throughput, failing acceptance criteria.

Assumption: Material properties (density, particle size distribution) remain within a known range during evaluation. Risk: If material composition changes significantly, sensor calibration may become invalid.

Assumption: Evaluation period is long enough to average transient effects (startup, shutdown, process variations). Risk: Short evaluation windows may not capture representative steady-state throughput.

Assumption: Acceptance criteria `≥ productivity_target` is defined as a single scalar threshold. Risk: If target is ambiguous or context-dependent (e.g., varies by material type or operating mode), measurement interpretation may be disputed.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan permits sensor installation and does not restrict measurement locations. Risk: Safety constraints may force suboptimal sensor placement, reducing measurement accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR to explicitly state: (1) whether throughput is reported in mass per unit time or volume per unit time (or both), (2) the required measurement accuracy (e.g., ±5% of target), (3) the minimum evaluation period duration, (4) whether material properties are assumed constant or variable, and (5) whether real-time reporting is required or batch reporting is acceptable. Additionally, establish a secondary validation method (e.g., manual tally or reference measurement) to verify DP1 performance against acceptance criteria before full deployment. Consider DP4 (multi-modal measurement with cross-validation) as a risk mitigation strategy if single-sensor reliability is uncertain.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and logical boundary encompassing all onboard sensing, actuation, and real-time data acquisition systems including flow rate measurement hardware, sensor integration, signal conditioning, and cumulative throughput computation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle transmits instantaneous flow rate and cumulative throughput measurements to external systems (NGHE Solution, Teleoperation) at periodic intervals

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives evaluation period parameters, sensor calibration commands, and flow measurement mode directives from external control authority

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle uses network link to exchange telemetry and tasking messages with remote systems; may also receive firmware updates for sensor firmware

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment: continuous sensing hardware, signal conditioning, integration logic, and cumulative computation are all physically and logically resident on the vehicle. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry output and tasking input cross boundary; no algorithmic or data logic leaks to other modules.

Poor containment: NGHE Solution is a high-level orchestration/planning layer. Placing real-time sensor acquisition and integration here violates separation of concerns. Sensor hardware cannot reside in NGHE; only post-processed results would be available. High diffusion: raw sensor data, calibration logic, and real-time computation would need to diffuse back to Vehicle.

Poor containment: Teleoperation is a remote human-in-the-loop control interface, not a data acquisition platform. Sensor hardware and continuous integration cannot be hosted remotely. High diffusion: all sensing and computation must occur on Vehicle; Teleoperation would only receive and display results.

Poor containment: Safety Services is a cross-cutting concern module for hazard detection and mitigation, not a primary data acquisition system. Flow rate sensing is a functional capability, not a safety function per se. High diffusion: sensor data and integration logic must reside elsewhere; Safety Services would only consume flow rate telemetry for threshold monitoring.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module is the natural and necessary boundary for DP1 because (1) continuous sensing hardware must be physically mounted on the vehicle platform, (2) real-time signal conditioning and integration require low-latency local computation, (3) cumulative throughput state must be maintained on the vehicle to ensure continuity across communication outages, and (4) the DP is a core operational capability of the vehicle itself, not a service provided by external systems.
containment_risks: Risk: If sensor calibration or integration algorithm parameters must be updated frequently by remote operators, the Tasking interface may become a bottleneck or require high-frequency updates, increasing coupling to Teleoperation or NGHE Solution.

Risk: If multiple external systems (NGHE, Teleoperation, Safety Services) each require different flow rate metrics (instantaneous vs. cumulative, different time windows), the Vehicle module may need to compute and export multiple variants, increasing complexity and potential for inconsistency.

Risk: If the evaluation period is dynamically variable and set by external tasking, the Vehicle must maintain state machines to track period boundaries, introducing potential for synchronization errors if tasking commands arrive out-of-order or are lost.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The Vehicle IM boundary is the natural locus for real-time flow rate acquisition and integration. Acceptance criteria (reported productivity ≥ productivity_target over agreed evaluation period) can be verified by instrumenting the Vehicle module to log instantaneous flow rate samples and cumulative throughput at period boundaries, then comparing against ground truth or reference measurements during representative operations. Telemetry interface provides the mechanism to export results for external validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. The FR is allocated entirely to the Vehicle IM. The FR does not require decomposition across multiple modules; all functional content (continuous sensing, signal conditioning, integration, cumulative computation) is contained within Vehicle's scope. External systems (NGHE, Teleoperation, Safety Services) consume the output (telemetry) but do not participate in the core DP realization.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP1 directly and sufficiently addresses the FR. The DP specifies a concrete mechanism (continuous sensing of material stream properties + integration over evaluation period) that logically solves the problem of measuring and reporting throughput in volumetric or mass units per unit time. The DP is technology-neutral and does not over-constrain the solution (e.g., it does not mandate a specific sensor type). Traceability is clear: FR → DP1 → IM (Vehicle).

diffusion_risks: Risk: Evaluation period parameter is externally tasked. If the period is dynamically variable and tasking commands arrive out-of-order, are delayed, or are lost, the Vehicle module must implement robust state machine logic to handle period boundary transitions. Recommend: Define a clear protocol for period updates (e.g., atomic period-start and period-end commands with sequence numbers) and specify Vehicle behavior on tasking loss (e.g., continue current period or revert to default).

Risk: Sensor calibration commands are received via Tasking interface. If calibration parameters must be updated frequently or in response to environmental drift, the Vehicle-to-external-control coupling increases. Recommend: Establish a calibration update frequency SLA and define whether calibration is applied retroactively to in-flight integration or only to future measurements.

Risk: Multiple external systems may request different flow rate metrics (instantaneous vs. cumulative, different aggregation windows, different units). The Vehicle module may need to compute and export multiple variants, increasing internal complexity and risk of metric inconsistency. Recommend: Define a canonical set of exported metrics (e.g., instantaneous flow rate + cumulative throughput at period end) and require external systems to derive secondary metrics from these canonical outputs rather than requesting custom computations from Vehicle.

Risk: Cumulative throughput state is maintained on Vehicle. If the vehicle loses power or communication during an evaluation period, state continuity depends on non-volatile storage. Recommend: Specify whether cumulative state is persisted to non-volatile memory and how recovery is handled if the vehicle restarts mid-period.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score). All hardware (sensors, signal conditioning), real-time computation (integration logic), and state (cumulative throughput) are resident on the Vehicle module. The DP is not diffused across multiple IM boundaries. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) are narrow and well-defined: Telemetry exports results, Tasking imports control parameters, Network Link is the transport. No algorithmic or data logic leaks to NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. The Vehicle module is the sole owner of DP1 realization.

recommended_revisions: Clarify the evaluation period protocol: Define whether the period is static (set once at mission start) or dynamic (updated via Tasking). If dynamic, specify the command format, sequence numbering, and Vehicle behavior on command loss or out-of-order arrival.

Define the canonical set of exported metrics: Specify whether Vehicle exports instantaneous flow rate, cumulative throughput, period boundaries, or all three. Establish a versioning or schema mechanism for telemetry to support future metric additions without breaking external consumers.

Specify sensor calibration update semantics: Define whether calibration parameter updates are applied retroactively to in-flight integration or only to future measurements. Establish a calibration update frequency SLA to bound the Tasking interface coupling.

Address cumulative state persistence: Specify whether cumulative throughput is persisted to non-volatile storage on the Vehicle and how recovery is handled if the vehicle restarts mid-period (e.g., resume from last checkpoint, reset to zero, or flag as invalid).

Define acceptance criteria operationally: The current acceptance criterion (reported productivity ≥ productivity_target) is outcome-focused but does not specify the measurement method or reference standard. Recommend: Add a verification plan that specifies how productivity_target is established (e.g., via ground truth measurement, reference sensor, or calibrated baseline) and how reported values are validated during representative operations.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, and containment is excellent (0.92). However, the safety criticality context (high) combined with four material diffusion risks and five recommended revisions creates unacceptable ambiguity in the DP and IM specifications. The risks are not showstoppers—they are resolvable—but they must be addressed before proceeding to sub-level decomposition or detailed design. Specifically: (1) the evaluation period protocol is underspecified (static vs. dynamic, command sequencing, loss handling); (2) the canonical telemetry schema is not defined; (3) sensor calibration update semantics are unclear; (4) cumulative state persistence and recovery are not addressed; and (5) acceptance criteria lack an operational verification plan. In a high-safety-criticality system, these ambiguities propagate downstream and increase rework risk. Revising the DP and IM specifications now to resolve these points will yield a clearer, more robust baseline for sub-level decomposition.

revise.why: The DP (DP1: Continuously sense material stream properties and integrate over evaluation period to compute and report throughput) is logically sound but operationally underspecified. The revision must clarify: (a) whether the evaluation period is static or dynamic, and if dynamic, the command protocol and Vehicle state machine behavior; (b) the canonical set of exported metrics (instantaneous flow rate, cumulative throughput, period boundaries, units, precision); (c) sensor calibration update semantics (retroactive vs. forward-only, update frequency SLA); (d) cumulative state persistence and recovery strategy (non-volatile storage, checkpoint mechanism, restart behavior); and (e) the operational verification plan for acceptance criteria (reference standard, measurement method, validation protocol). These clarifications should be captured as DP1 sub-specifications or as explicit assumptions/constraints in the DP statement. The revised DP should be unambiguous enough that an IM designer can implement it without requiring further interpretation.

assumptions: Evaluation period is assumed to be static (set once at mission start) unless explicitly tasked otherwise. If dynamic, the protocol must be defined before IM design.

Cumulative throughput state is assumed to be maintained in volatile memory on the Vehicle module. If non-volatile persistence is required for safety or mission continuity, this must be specified in the revised DP.

Sensor calibration is assumed to be applied only to future measurements (not retroactively to in-flight integration). If retroactive application is required, the DP must specify the mechanism and impact on reported metrics.

Telemetry export is assumed to include instantaneous flow rate and cumulative throughput at period boundaries. If additional metrics (e.g., sensor health, calibration status) are required, the schema must be defined.

Acceptance criteria are assumed to be verified against a reference measurement or ground truth during representative operations. The reference standard and validation protocol must be defined in the revised DP or in a separate verification plan.
open_risks: Evaluation period protocol ambiguity: If the period is dynamic and tasking commands arrive out-of-order, are delayed, or are lost, the Vehicle module behavior is undefined. Risk level: Medium. Mitigation: Define a clear state machine with sequence numbering and loss-handling rules.

Sensor calibration coupling: Frequent calibration updates via Tasking interface increase external control coupling and risk of metric inconsistency. Risk level: Medium. Mitigation: Establish a calibration update frequency SLA and define whether updates are applied retroactively or forward-only.

Cumulative state persistence: If the vehicle loses power or communication mid-period, state continuity depends on non-volatile storage, which is not specified. Risk level: Medium-High (in high-safety-criticality context). Mitigation: Specify persistence strategy and recovery behavior (resume, reset, or flag as invalid).

Telemetry schema evolution: Multiple external systems may request different flow rate metrics, risking metric inconsistency and internal complexity. Risk level: Low-Medium. Mitigation: Define a canonical set of exported metrics and require external systems to derive secondary metrics.

Acceptance criteria verification: The criterion (reported productivity ≥ productivity_target) is outcome-focused but does not specify the measurement method or reference standard. Risk level: Medium. Mitigation: Define an operational verification plan with reference standard and validation protocol.
evidence_needed: Specification of evaluation period protocol: Is the period static or dynamic? If dynamic, provide the command format, sequence numbering scheme, and Vehicle state machine behavior on command loss or out-of-order arrival.

Definition of canonical telemetry schema: Specify the set of exported metrics (instantaneous flow rate, cumulative throughput, period boundaries, units, precision, timestamp format). Include versioning or schema evolution mechanism.

Sensor calibration update semantics: Define whether calibration parameter updates are applied retroactively to in-flight integration or only to future measurements. Establish a calibration update frequency SLA.

Cumulative state persistence strategy: Specify whether cumulative throughput is persisted to non-volatile storage on the Vehicle. If yes, define the checkpoint mechanism and recovery behavior on restart (resume from checkpoint, reset to zero, or flag as invalid).

Operational verification plan: Define how productivity_target is established (ground truth measurement, reference sensor, or calibrated baseline). Specify the measurement method, reference standard, and validation protocol for representative operations.

Safety-critical failure modes analysis: Given high safety criticality, conduct FMEA or similar analysis on the DP realization to identify failure modes (sensor failure, calibration drift, state loss, communication loss) and specify mitigation strategies in the revised DP.
